turns.user_message: I used to handle my own slip-ups without a second thought, but lately I keep replaying every minor mistake in my head. This heavy feeling just started recently, and it catches me completely off guard. I catch myself staring at the wall wondering why I can't just move on like I always used to. It feels like I'm carrying an invisible weight over absolutely nothing.

I never used to overthink my own presence, but now I constantly second guess every word I say out loud. This shift came out of nowhere, and it has completely taken over my daily routine. I spend hours dissecting simple interactions, convinced that I accidentally crossed some invisible line. It is exhausting to feel like I am constantly apologizing for just existing. I wish I could just flip a switch and go back to how I used to be.

I used to trust my own judgment without hesitation, but lately I feel completely poisoned by my own choices. This sudden wave of regret just keeps building, and I cannot figure out how to stop it. Every minor decision feels like a glaring failure that proves I am fundamentally broken. I keep waiting for the old version of myself to return, but the guilt just sits heavier each day. It is so frustrating to watch myself shrink into someone I barely recognize.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This just started recently.
